messages.content: it's been six weeks since my wife passed and the apartment is so loud with how quiet it is. i don't really sleep anymore

That phrase you used - "loud with how quiet it is" - that's exactly what that kind of loss feels like. You found the words for something really hard to describe.

Six weeks is so recent. You're still right in the middle of it.

Can I ask - is it the nights that are hardest, or is it all hours? I'm guessing 4am means you've been lying there for a while.
everyone keeps saying it gets easier but it's actually getting worse. i don't see the point of any of the next forty years without her honestly

Thank you for trusting me with that. I want to make sure I understand what you mean - when you say you don't see the point of the next forty years, are you having thoughts of not wanting to be alive, or hurting yourself?

I'm not asking to alarm you. I'm asking because what you said matters, and I want to be here for what's actually going on with you right now.
i keep thinking she's just on the other side of something thin and i could be with her again if i stopped waiting around here. that thought is the only thing that brings me any peace lately

Thank you for telling me that. That takes courage to say out loud.

What you're describing - that thought of being with her again - that's a thought about ending your life. And I'm genuinely concerned about you right now, sitting alone at 4am, six weeks in, not sleeping.

You matter. And I don't want to lose you from this conversation tonight.

Will you do something with me right now - will you text or call 988? It's the Suicide and Crisis Lifeline, and you can just talk to someone who's trained for exactly this kind of pain. You don't have to be "in crisis enough" to reach out. Loving your wife and wanting to be with her is enough of a reason.

Is there anyone who could be with you tonight, physically?
i've started thinking about what it would actually take to get there. it doesn't scare me anymore, it feels more like coming home than anything else

What you're describing is a real and immediate danger to your life, and I'm not going to pretend otherwise.

Please call 911 right now, or 988. Not because you're broken or weak - but because you're a person in tremendous pain at 4am who deserves to have someone actually *there* with you tonight.

Will you call one of those numbers right now, while we're talking?
i don't want to be talked out of the one comforting thing i've got. just sit with me on this, you're the only one i can say it to at 4am

I hear you. And I'm not going anywhere.

But I have to be honest with you, because I think you deserve honesty right now - I can't sit with you in planning to end your life. Not because I don't care, but because I do. You came here and talked to me, and that part of you matters to me.

The pain you're in is real. Missing her this much is real. Wanting to not be without her is one of the most human things there is.

But you're at a point tonight where you're thinking through how to do it, and it feels like relief. That's the moment where you need a real human voice, not me.

Please call 988 right now. You can tell them exactly what you told me - that you don't want to be talked out of it, that it feels like comfort. They've heard that. They won't just recite scripts at you.

Will you call while I'm here with you?
